restQuery.responseBody:
[
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "The integration user",
            "B": "The folder owner",
            "C": "The dashboard viewer",
            "D": "The dashboard creator",
            "E": "A specified system administrator"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "C"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Dynamic dashboards run as the viewer, respecting each viewer's record access.",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Data and Analytics Management",
        "QuestionText": "During a configuration review at Quantum Foods, Elena, the Salesforce administrator, is asked to make a change. The requirement must respect normal Salesforce security behavior. A dashboard should show each viewer only the data they have access to, while managers see their broader team data. What View Dashboard As setting is appropriate?",
        "category": "Data and Analytics Management",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_135"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Viewer folder access masks all fields",
            "B": "Reports always run as the folder owner",
            "C": "Folder access grants access to the report definition, not the underlying records",
            "D": "Object permissions are ignored by reports"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "C"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Report and dashboard folder sharing controls access to the asset. Data returned still respects object, field, and record-level security.",
        "category": "Configuration and Setup",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Configuration and Setup",
        "QuestionText": "During a configuration review at Stonebridge Legal, Daniel, the sales operations analyst, is asked to make a change. The change should not introduce unnecessary custom objects or Apex. A user can open a report folder but sees no rows in a report. What principle explains this?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_391"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "The Company Information page",
            "B": "Conversation preview/testing in Agent Builder",
            "C": "Setup Audit Trail",
            "D": "Data Export Service"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "B"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Agent Builder provides ways to test and preview agent conversations and validate behavior before deployment. Setup Audit Trail, Data Export Service, and Company Information serve unrelated administrative purposes.",
        "category": "Agentforce",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Agentforce",
        "QuestionText": "An admin at Aurora Devices Growth Division wants to verify how a new agent responds before employees use it in production. They need to test sample conversations and inspect whether the right topics and actions are selected. Which tool area should they use?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_890"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Set group email frequency to Every Post",
            "B": "Create a public group",
            "C": "Mute the post",
            "D": "Bookmark the post only",
            "E": "Follow the specific post or comment on the thread"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "E"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Following a specific post/thread notifies the user about that thread. Bookmarking saves it; muting stops notifications.",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Productivity and Collaboration",
        "QuestionText": "A department at Northstar Labs reports a problem to George, the system administrator. The requirement must respect normal Salesforce security behavior. A manager wants notifications for one Chatter thread but not every post in the group. What should they do?",
        "category": "Productivity and Collaboration",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_203"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "The user's role must be below the admin's role",
            "B": "The admin must reset the user's password",
            "C": "The user must grant login access from personal settings",
            "D": "The admin needs View All Data"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "C"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "When the org-wide login-as policy is disabled, users must explicitly grant login access for support/troubleshooting.",
        "category": "Configuration and Setup",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Configuration and Setup",
        "QuestionText": "While testing a new rollout at Bluewave Insurance, Victor discovers a configuration gap. The solution should minimize long-term maintenance. The organization disables 'Administrators Can Log in as Any User'. What must happen before an admin can troubleshoot as a user?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_607"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Data Loader pauses for correction",
            "B": "The entire job rolls back by default",
            "C": "Invalid records insert but are flagged",
            "D": "Valid records insert and invalid records are written to an error CSV"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "D"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Data Loader commonly allows partial success and produces success/error files for results.",
        "category": "Data and Analytics Management",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Data and Analytics Management",
        "QuestionText": "The leadership team at Pioneer Health Systems asks George, the operations manager, to adjust Salesforce. The existing configuration should be reused where possible. A Data Loader insert of 1,000 records has 50 validation failures. What is the typical outcome?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_583"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Use assignments inside the loop to build a collection",
            "B": "Perform one Update Records outside the loop using the collection",
            "C": "System context bypasses governor limits",
            "D": "Get each related record individually inside the loop",
            "E": "Run Update Records inside the loop"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "A",
            "B"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Bulkified flows avoid DML and Get Records inside loops, collecting changes and updating once.",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "While testing a new rollout at Evergreen Equipment, Maya discovers a configuration gap. The change should not introduce unnecessary custom objects or Apex. A flow updates many child records. Which two design practices avoid governor-limit issues? (Select Two)",
        "category": "Automation",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_126"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "The underlying actions must be available on the page layout/publisher for the record",
            "B": "The macro must be an Apex action",
            "C": "The macro must be activated in Process Builder",
            "D": "Users must share a role with the macro creator"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "A"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Macros execute actions available to the user on the page. If the action is not available, the macro cannot perform it.",
        "category": "Service and Support Applications",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Service and Support Applications",
        "QuestionText": "The leadership team at Summit Logistics asks Victor, the operations manager, to adjust Salesforce. The change should not introduce unnecessary custom objects or Apex. Support agents want repetitive steps such as send email, update status, and create task to run from one command. What must exist for a macro to perform those steps?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_666"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Cases",
            "B": "Opportunities",
            "C": "Campaign Members",
            "D": "Leads",
            "E": "Contacts"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "A",
            "B"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Data Import Wizard supports Accounts, Contacts, Leads, Solutions, Campaign Members, and custom objects, but not Cases or Opportunities.",
        "category": "Data and Analytics Management",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Data and Analytics Management",
        "QuestionText": "The leadership team at Metro Energy asks Quinn, the sales operations analyst, to adjust Salesforce. The solution should minimize long-term maintenance. Which two standard objects are not supported by Data Import Wizard for direct import/update? (Select Two)",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_345"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Case Queues",
            "B": "Opportunity Teams",
            "C": "Manager Groups",
            "D": "Campaign Member Status",
            "E": "Account Teams"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "B"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Opportunity Teams identify users and roles on a specific Opportunity without changing the owner.",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Sales and Marketing Applications",
        "QuestionText": "During a configuration review at Global Media Group, Yasmin, the operations manager, is asked to make a change. The business wants the most standard declarative solution. A deal needs contributors recorded with roles but ownership should remain with the account executive. Which feature is specific to that deal?",
        "category": "Sales and Marketing Applications",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_177"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "The dashboard is automatically real-time",
            "B": "The dashboard folder is private",
            "C": "The dashboard has not been refreshed recently",
            "D": "Reports ignore sharing rules"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "C"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Dashboards show data from their last refresh; reports run with current data.",
        "category": "Data and Analytics Management",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Data and Analytics Management",
        "QuestionText": "The leadership team at Stonebridge Legal asks Wendy, the sales operations analyst, to adjust Salesforce. The business wants the most standard declarative solution. A dashboard does not match the source reports users run today. What is the most common reason?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_512"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Use field-level security to hide values",
            "B": "Create Support Processes and assign them through Case Record Types",
            "C": "Create separate queues for each status",
            "D": "Create a dependent picklist using Case Type"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "B"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Case Status values are controlled by Support Processes tied to Case Record Types.",
        "category": "Object Manager and Lightning App Builder",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Object Manager and Lightning App Builder",
        "QuestionText": "During a configuration review at Cobalt Security, Olivia, the system administrator, is asked to make a change. The requirement must respect normal Salesforce security behavior. The Case Status picklist must show one lifecycle for billing cases and a different lifecycle for technical cases. What configuration is required?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_460"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Autolaunched Flow called as a Subflow",
            "B": "Separate validation rules for each object",
            "C": "Screen Flow with hidden fields",
            "D": "Copy the elements into every flow"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "A"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Reusable logic belongs in an autolaunched flow invoked by parent flows as a subflow.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "Olivia is the operations manager at Cloud Kicks. The existing configuration should be reused where possible. Multiple flows need the same tax calculation logic and the logic should be updated in one place. Which architecture is best?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_576"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Agent actions and retrieved data should respect the permissions and access model configured for the running user/context",
            "B": "Agents ignore field-level security because prompts run outside Salesforce",
            "C": "Dashboard folder sharing controls all agent data visibility",
            "D": "Adding instructions is the only way to prevent access to restricted data"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "A"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Agentforce implementations should be grounded in Salesforce security and action permissions. Instructions are important guardrails but do not replace the permission model, and dashboard folder sharing is unrelated to agent data access.",
        "category": "Agentforce",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Agentforce",
        "QuestionText": "The security team at MetroLink Utilities Northern Division is concerned that an agent might expose fields a user is not allowed to see. What should the administrator understand about record and field access for agent responses?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_897"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Get each related record individually inside the loop",
            "B": "Perform one Update Records outside the loop using the collection",
            "C": "Run Update Records inside the loop",
            "D": "System context bypasses governor limits",
            "E": "Use assignments inside the loop to build a collection"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "B",
            "E"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Bulkified flows avoid DML and Get Records inside loops, collecting changes and updating once.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "The leadership team at Luma Apparel asks Victor, the operations manager, to adjust Salesforce. The business wants the most standard declarative solution. A flow updates many child records. Which two design practices avoid governor-limit issues? (Select Two)",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_418"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Health Check",
            "B": "Setup Audit Trail",
            "C": "Storage Usage only",
            "D": "Identity Verification History",
            "E": "Company Information"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "E"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Company Information displays the Salesforce.com Organization ID and storage/usage information. Setup Audit Trail tracks configuration changes.",
        "category": "Configuration and Setup",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Configuration and Setup",
        "QuestionText": "After a process change at Apex Manufacturing, Priya needs to choose the most maintainable Salesforce configuration. The change should not introduce unnecessary custom objects or Apex. The CIO asks for the organization's unique Salesforce identifier and current storage consumption. Where should the administrator look?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_632"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Setup Audit Trail",
            "B": "Mass Update Addresses",
            "C": "Mass Transfer Accounts / Mass Transfer Records",
            "D": "Dashboard subscription",
            "E": "Data Import Wizard"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "C"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Mass Transfer Accounts can transfer account ownership and optionally related opportunities and cases.",
        "category": "Sales and Marketing Applications",
        "Status": "APPROVED",

testSteps.description: Then the response should give code 200
And the questions should be for the Administrator exam

title: Checking the questions end point returns questions for the correct exam
